content.full_text:
I
Fnrn Polrrrcar- PRACTTcEs CouvrrssroN
.128 J Street r Suite 620 r Sacramento. CA 95811-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
January 25,2008
Kate Garay
19 Sunny Oaks Drive
San Rafael, CA949O3
RE: Your Facsimile Request of l2ll5l07
Our File No. W-07-196
Dear Ms. Garay:
This letter responds to your inquiry regarding provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act") I that may apply to service as a Commissioner for a California State Government
advisory body. [n our telephone conversation of December 26,2007, I explained that the
Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") provides written advice to "any
person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that person's authorized
representative." The Commission, however, does not provide formal written advice unless
the name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are
provided. (Regulation 18329 (b)(zXA). )
The Commission may also provide informal assistance, either orally or in writing.
(See Regulation 18329(c).) Howevero "the Commission may decline to provide informal
assistance or may limit such assistance to the explanation in general terms, of the
requirements of the Act in any of the following circumstances:"
"(C) Assistance or advice is being sought regarding the duties of
another person and the requestor does not appear to be authorized to make the
request as the person's representative, or does not provide the identity of the
person on whose behalf the assistance is being sought." (Regulation
r832e(c)@Xc).)
As we discussed in our telephone conversation, the Commissioner on whose
behalf you made your inquiry may contact us for written or oral advice. Additionally,
with respect to your question, you may wish to review Regulation 18702.4 (aX2).
I The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014, All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.


File No. W-07-196
Page No. 2
Commission regulations may be accessed on the web at www.l'ppc.ca.gov under the
library and publications link.
For the reasons stated above, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Counsel
By:
ir
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
wJLjgl

sections.conclusion: For the reasons stated above, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked about provisions of the Political Reform Act that may apply to service as a Commissioner for a California State Government advisory body, but sought advice on behalf of another person without providing required identifying information.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the requestor sought assistance regarding the duties of another person without being authorized as that person's representative or providing the person's identity, which falls under Regulation 18329(c)(3)(C) as a circumstance where the Commission may decline informal assistance.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections; legal basis and explanation for declination found in body text. References to specific regulations provided. Requestor instructed that the Commissioner may contact FPPC directly for advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor asked about provisions of the Political Reform Act that may apply to service as a Commissioner for a California State Government advisory body, but sought advice on behalf of another person without providing required identifying information.

CONCLUSION: For the reasons stated above, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC declination letter refusing to provide written advice to Kate Garay regarding Political Reform Act duties for a state government advisory commissioner position, because she requested advice on behalf of another person without proper authorization or identification.
